briefdescription.para: Become true if bridge creation API called at least 1 time.
Become false if bridge destruction API called at least 1 time.
Constructor.
Destructor.
Adds the accessible object associated with given actorId to the brige.

parameterdescription.para: The actorId assosiated with the accessible

simplesect.para: true if given accessible is added to the bridge

briefdescription.para: Removed the accessible object associated with given actorId from the brige.
Gets the accessible object associated with given actor from the bridge.
Gets the accessible object associated with given path from the bridge.
Returns true if GetChildren should include hidden objects; false otherwise.
Gets bus name which bridge is initialized on.
Registers top level window.

detaileddescription.para: Hierarchy of objects visible for accessibility clients is based on tree-like structure created from Actors objects. This method allows to connect chosen object as direct ancestor of application and therefore make it visible for accessibility clients.

briefdescription.para: Removes top level window.

detaileddescription.para: Hierarchy of objects visible for accessibility clients is based on tree-like structure created from Actors objects. This method removes previously added window from visible accessibility objects.

briefdescription.para: Adds object on the top of the stack of "default label" sourcing objects.

parameterdescription.para: The actor to set a default label

briefdescription.para: Removes object from the stack of "default label" sourcing objects.

parameterdescription.para: The actor to unset a default label

briefdescription.para: Gets the top-most object from the stack of "default label" sourcing objects.

detaileddescription.para: The "default label" is a reading material (text) derived from an accesibility object that could be read by screen-reader immediately after the navigation context has changed (window activates, popup shows up, tab changes) and before first UI element is highlighted.

parameterdescription.para: The root of the navigation context for which to retrieve the default label.

simplesect.para: This is a Tizen only feature not present in upstream ATSPI. Feature can be enabled/disabled for particular context root object by setting value of its accessibility attribute "default_label". Following strings are valid values for "default_label" attribute: "enabled", "disabled". Any other value will be interpreted as "enabled".

briefdescription.para: Sets name of current application which will be visible on accessibility bus.
Sets the name of the GUI toolkit that AT-SPI clients can query.
Gets object being root of accessibility tree.
Finds an object in accessibility tree.
Notifies accessibility dbus that window has just been created.
Notifies accessibility dbus that window has just been shown.
Notifies accessibility dbus that window has just been hidden.
Notifies accessibility dbus that window has just been focused.
Notifies accessibility dbus that window has just been out of focus.

parameterdescription.para: The window to be out of focus

briefdescription.para: Notifies accessibility dbus that window has just been minimized.
Notifies accessibility dbus that window has just been restored.
Notifies accessibility dbus that window has just been maximized.
Tells the bridge that the application is not running.
Tells the bridge that the application is running.
Initializes accessibility bus.
Terminates accessibility bus.
This method is called, when bridge is being activated.
This method is called, when bridge is being deactivated.
Checks if bridge is activated or not.
Emits cursor-moved event on at-spi bus.
Emits active-descendant-changed event on at-spi bus.

memberdef.argsstring: (Accessible *obj, TextChangedState state, unsigned int position, unsigned int length, const std::string &content)=0
memberdef.name: EmitTextChanged

briefdescription.para: Emits text-changed event on at-spi bus.

parameterdescription.para: The changed state for text, such as Inserted or Deleted

briefdescription.para: Emits MoveOuted event on at-spi bus.

parameterdescription.para: Direction type when an Accessible object moves out of screen

briefdescription.para: Emits ScrollStarted event on at-spi bus.
Emits ScrollFinished event on at-spi bus.

memberdef.argsstring: (std::shared_ptr< Accessible > obj, State state, int newValue, int reserved=0)=0
memberdef.name: EmitStateChanged

briefdescription.para: Emits state-changed event on at-spi bus.

parameterdescription.para: Whether the state value is changed to new value or not.
Reserved. (Currently, this argument is not implemented in dali)

briefdescription.para: Emits window event on at-spi bus.

parameterdescription.para: The additional parameter which interpretation depends on chosen event

briefdescription.para: Emits property-changed event on at-spi bus.
Emits bounds-changed event on at-spi bus.
Emits org.a11y.atspi.Event.Window.PostRender on the AT-SPI bus.

detaileddescription.para:
obj
The Accessible sender object
The sender of this event is expected to be an Accessible object that represents a top-level window.
The actual number of events emitted during a given time interval may be smaller than the number of calls to this method, but at least one is guaranteed.

briefdescription.para: Emits key event on at-spi bus.

detaileddescription.para: Screen-reader might receive this event and reply, that given keycode is consumed. In that case further processing of the keycode should be ignored.

parameterdescription.para: Notification if the event was consumed

simplesect.para: true if the event was emitted

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::string &text, bool discardable, std::function< void(std::string)> callback)=0
memberdef.name: Say

briefdescription.para: Reads given text by screen reader.

parameterdescription.para: If TRUE, reading can be discarded by subsequent reading requests, if FALSE the reading must finish before next reading request can be started

the callback function that is called on reading signals emitted during processing of this reading request. Callback can be one of the following signals: ReadingCancelled, ReadingStopped, ReadingSkipped

briefdescription.para: Force accessibility client to pause.
Force accessibility client to resume.
Cancels anything screen-reader is reading / has queued to read.

parameterdescription.para: whether to cancel non-discardable readings as well

briefdescription.para: Suppresses reading of screen-reader.

parameterdescription.para: whether to suppress reading of screen-reader

briefdescription.para: Gets screen reader status.

simplesect.para: True if screen reader is enabled

briefdescription.para: Gets ATSPI status.
Calls socket.Embed(plug) via D-Bus.

simplesect.para: Address returned by the D-Bus call.
Remote object pointed to by 'socket' must implement 'org.a11y.atspi.Socket'.

briefdescription.para: Calls socket.Unmbed(plug) via D-Bus.

simplesect.para: Remote object pointed to by 'socket' must implement 'org.a11y.atspi.Socket'.

briefdescription.para: Calls socket.SetOffset(x, y) via D-Bus.

detaileddescription.para: The "SetOffset" D-Bus method is a DALi extension. It can be used to inform a DALi widget about its position on the screen.

simplesect.para: Remote object pointed to by 'socket' must implement 'org.a11y.atspi.Socket'.

briefdescription.para: Sets the global extents offset.

detaileddescription.para: This offset will be added during serialization of GetExtents() return value to D-Bus. Local calls to GetExtents() are unaffected.

briefdescription.para: Sets the preferred bus name.

detaileddescription.para: If the Bridge is enabled, it will immediately release the previous name and request the new one.
Otherwise, the Bridge will request this name on AT-SPI activation (and release it on deactivation). It is up to the caller to determine whether a given name will be available in the system.

briefdescription.para: Returns instance of bridge singleton object.
Blocks auto-initialization of AT-SPI bridge.

detaileddescription.para: Use this only if your application starts before DBus does, and call it early in main() (before GetCurrentBridge() is called by anyone). GetCurrentBridge() will then return an instance of DummyBridge.

When DBus is ready, call EnableAutoInit(). Please note that GetCurrentBridge() may still return an instance of DummyBridge if AT-SPI was disabled at compile time or using an environment variable, or if creating the real bridge failed.

briefdescription.para: Re-enables auto-initialization of AT-SPI bridge.

detaileddescription.para: Normal applications do not have to call this function. GetCurrentBridge() tries to initialize the AT-SPI bridge when it is called for the first time.

briefdescription.para: Encodes a widget ID as a usable bus name.

parameterdescription.para: The instance ID of a widget
The process ID (PID) of the widget

briefdescription.para: Tells bridge, that given object is considered root (doesn't have any parents).

detaileddescription.para: All root objects will have the same parent - application object. Application object is controlled by bridge and private.

briefdescription.para: Base class for different accessibility bridges.

detaileddescription.para: Bridge is resposible for initializing and managing connection on accessibility bus. Accessibility clients will not get any information about UI without initialized and upraised bridge. Concrete implementation depends on the accessibility technology available on the platform.
